subject: Social Science
learning_outcomes: Analyze the main points of consumer protection laws
Understand the methods of filing a complaint in consumer court

key_terms.definition: The so-called 'king of the market' — the person who purchases goods and services
Act enacted in 1986 to protect consumers' interests
Buying things sitting at home through information technology
March 15 — because US President John F. Kennedy signed Consumer Protection Act on March 15, 1962
Consumer court where complaints with compensation less than one crore are filed

mcqs.question: World Consumer Day is observed on
mcqs.explanation: World Consumer Rights Day is observed on March 15 every year

mcqs.question: The so-called king of the market is the
mcqs.explanation: The consumer is called the king of the market

mcqs.question: Complaints where the compensation amount is less than one crore should be filed with
mcqs.explanation: Complaints for compensation less than ₹1 crore are filed with the District Consumer Commission

mcqs.question: In which year did the Consumer Protection Act come into force?
mcqs.explanation: The Consumer Protection Act came into force in 1986

one_mark_questions.question: What is teleshopping?
one_mark_questions.answer: With the development of information technology, today we can buy things sitting at home — this is called teleshopping

one_mark_questions.question: What is the need of consumer protection?
one_mark_questions.answer: Protecting consumers from exploitation by manufacturers and traders

one_mark_questions.question: Why is World Consumer Rights Day celebrated on 15th March?
one_mark_questions.answer: Because US President John F. Kennedy signed the Consumer Protection Act on March 15, 1962, granting four basic rights to consumers

three_mark_questions.question: What are the rights given by Consumer Protection Act to consumers?
answer: The right to be protected against sale of dangerous goods
The right to choose the goods
The right to be heard about the goods
The right to seek redress against exploitation
The right against fraud or deception
